title: QA Integer-State Pipeline: two-crossings-only, integer interior, recomputed-deterministic
source_attribution: GLM-5 Team, arXiv:2602.15763 [cs.LG], Feb 2026 — §4.1.2 Token-In-Token-Out (TITO) gateway. Re-tokenization on the learner side corrupted action↔reward alignment. This cert certifies the QA-native structural guarantee: observer/QA boundary crossed exactly twice (Theorem NT), integer tuples throughout interior, no continuous intermediate — the TITO failure mode is structurally impossible. Reference prototype qa_lab/qa_orbit_resonance_attention.py provides the canonical input/evolve/output pipeline. Design doc docs/theory/QA_GLM5_ARCHITECTURE_MAPPING.md §Failure-Mode-2. Connects [256] QA Orbit-Resonance Attention (middle layer), [159] QA Observer Core (qa_mod determinism), [129] QA Projection Obstruction. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-19.
theorem_statement: A QA-native pipeline crosses the observer/QA boundary exactly twice. All interior state between the two crossings is an integer tuple (b, e) in {1..m}^2. No re-tokenization through a continuous intermediate occurs inside the pipeline. The pipeline output is deterministic: repeated invocations on identical input produce bitwise-identical integer interior traces.

input_boundary.description: Continuous input signal → integer tuples (b, e) in {1..m}^2 via domain-appropriate quantization. This is the first and only input-side boundary crossing.

output_boundary.description: Integer tuples → continuous output (e.g., float vector, decoded signal). Uses RAW d=b+e, a=b+2e per 2026-04-09 rule — elements use raw derived coords. This is the first and only output-side boundary crossing.

pipeline_stages.description: Orbit-resonance attention — deterministic pairwise resonance on T-orbit tuples, cert [256]
T-operator evolution on integer path time (T1)

determinism.note: Repeated invocation on the canonical seed tokens produces bitwise-identical interior trace. Structural reason: integer arithmetic + deterministic T-operator; no stochastic ops, no float state, no Adam buffers across integer-state transitions.

design_claim_context: GLM-5's TITO gateway is engineering discipline on top of a continuous-learning substrate; re-tokenization drift is possible in principle and was observed in practice. The QA-native pipeline certified here has no continuous intermediate to re-project through. The two-boundary-crossing invariant of Theorem NT is structurally enforced, not just documented.
